paragraphs: Article 58. The competent authority, the sectoral authorities that make up the SIA and the Permanent Council for Regulatory Cooperation on Artificial Intelligence (Cria), in cooperation with the Ministry of Labor, shall develop guidelines for, among other objectives:
I – mitigate potential negative impacts on workers, in particular the risks of job displacement and career opportunities related to AI;
II – enhance positive impacts on workers, especially to improve health and safety in the workplace;
III – value negotiation instruments and collective agreements;
IV – encourage the development of training programs and continuous training for active workers, promoting professional appreciation and improvement.
Article 59. Public administration within the scope of the Union, the States, the Federal District and the Municipalities may encourage innovation and productive and technological development in AI.
Sole paragraph. The development measures referred to in the chapeau shall be guided by the following guidelines:

I – promotion of innovation in the productive sectors, including through the contracting of innovative solutions by the State and the celebration of public-private partnerships under the terms of Law No. 11,079, of December 30, 2004;

II – investment in research for the development of AI in the country, focused on the Brazilian socioeconomic context, valuing the country's technological and data autonomy and its insertion and competitiveness in the domestic and international markets;
III – financing difficult-to-access physical and technological AI resources for small and medium-sized companies and research centers that promote sustainable practices;

IV – encouraging the expansion of the availability of sustainable data centers with high data processing capacity for AI systems, with the densification of this production chain and related digital services in Brazil, with the aim of supporting the productive sector and technical-scientific research and development;
V – encouraging the creation of multidisciplinary centers for research, development and innovation in AI.
Article 60. Public and private entities shall prioritize the use of AI systems and applications that aim to achieve energy efficiency and rationalize the consumption of natural resources.
units.fullText: Article 60. Public and private entities shall prioritize the use of AI systems and applications that aim to achieve energy efficiency and rationalize the consumption of natural resources.

paragraphs: Article 61. Cria, in cooperation with the Ministry of Environment and Climate Change, shall promote research and development of certification programs to reduce the environmental impact of AI systems.
units.fullText: Article 61. Cria, in cooperation with the Ministry of Environment and Climate Change, shall promote research and development of certification programs to reduce the environmental impact of AI systems.

paragraphs: Article 62. The AI developer who uses content protected by copyright and related rights shall inform about the protected content used in the development processes of AI systems, by publishing a summary on an easily accessible website, observing commercial and industrial secrets, in accordance with specific regulations.
Sole paragraph. For the purposes of this Chapter, development comprises the stages of mining, training, retraining, testing, validating and applying AI systems.

Article 62. The AI developer who uses content protected by copyright and related rights shall inform about the protected content used in the development processes of AI systems, by publishing a summary on an easily accessible website, observing commercial and industrial secrets, in accordance with specific regulations.

Sole paragraph. For the purposes of this Chapter, development comprises the stages of mining, training, retraining, testing, validating and applying AI systems.

paragraphs: Article 63. The automated use of protected content in text and data mining processes for the purposes of research and development of AI systems by scientific, research and educational organizations and institutions, museums, public archives and libraries does not constitute an offense against copyright and related rights, provided that the following conditions are observed:
I – access to the content was obtained lawfully;
II – does not have commercial purposes;

III – the use of content protected by copyright and related rights is made to the extent necessary for the objective to be achieved, without prejudice to the economic interests of the holders and without competition with the normal exploitation of protected works and content.

§ 1. Copies of copyrighted and related content used in AI systems shall be stored in secure conditions, and only for the time necessary to carry out the activity or for the specific purpose of verifying the results.
§ 2. The exhibition or dissemination of works and content protected by copyright and related rights used in the development of AI systems is prohibited.

§ 3. This Article does not apply to institutions that are linked to, affiliated with, or controlled by a for-profit entity that provides AI systems, or that have an equity relationship with such an entity.

§ 4. The chapeau of this Article applies to text and data mining by public or private entities in the context of AI systems used to combat civil and criminal violations of copyright and related rights.

Article 63. The automated use of protected content in text and data mining processes for the purposes of research and development of AI systems by scientific, research and educational organizations and institutions, museums, public archives and libraries does not constitute an offense against copyright and related rights, provided that the following conditions are observed:

I – access to the content was obtained lawfully;

II – does not have commercial purposes;

III – the use of content protected by copyright and related rights is made to the extent necessary for the objective to be achieved, without prejudice to the economic interests of the holders and without competition with the normal exploitation of protected works and content.

§ 1. Copies of copyrighted and related content used in AI systems shall be stored in secure conditions, and only for the time necessary to carry out the activity or for the specific purpose of verifying the results.

§ 2. The exhibition or dissemination of works and content protected by copyright and related rights used in the development of AI systems is prohibited.

§ 3. This Article does not apply to institutions that are linked to, affiliated with, or controlled by a for-profit entity that provides AI systems, or that have an equity relationship with such an entity.

§ 4. The chapeau of this Article applies to text and data mining by public or private entities in the context of AI systems used to combat civil and criminal violations of copyright and related rights.

paragraphs: Article 64. The holder of copyright and related rights may prohibit the use of content owned by them in the development of AI systems in cases not covered by Article 63 of this Law.

Sole paragraph. The prohibition of the use of protected works and content in the databases of an AI system subsequent to the training process does not exempt the AI agent from being liable for moral and material losses and damages, in accordance with applicable legislation.

Article 64. The holder of copyright and related rights may prohibit the use of content owned by them in the development of AI systems in cases not covered by Article 63 of this Law.

Sole paragraph. The prohibition of the use of protected works and content in the databases of an AI system subsequent to the training process does not exempt the AI agent from being liable for moral and material losses and damages, in accordance with applicable legislation.

paragraphs: Article 65. The AI agent who uses copyrighted and related content in mining processes, training or development of AI systems shall remunerate the holders of this content due to this use, and shall ensure:

I – that the holders of copyright and related rights have effective conditions to negotiate collectively, in accordance with Title VI of Law No. 9,610, of February 19, 1998 (Copyright Law), or directly the use of the contents of which they are the holders, being able to do so free of charge or for a fee;

II – that the calculation of the remuneration referred to in the chapeau considers the principles of reasonableness and proportionality and relevant elements, such as the size of the AI agent and the competitive effects of the results in relation to the original content used;

III – freedom of negotiation in the use of protected content, with a view to promoting a research and experimentation environment conducive to innovative practices and without restricting freedom of contract among the parties involved, in accordance with Articles 156, 157, 421, 422, 478, and 479 of Law No. 10,406 of 10 January 2002 (Civil Code), and Article 4 of Law No. 9,610 of 19 February 1998 (Copyright Law).
§ 1. The remuneration referred to in the chapeau of this article is only due:
I – to holders of national or foreign copyright and related rights domiciled in Brazil;

II – to persons domiciled in a country that affords reciprocal protection, on terms equivalent to those of this Article, to the copyright and related rights of Brazilians, as provided in Article 2, sole paragraph, and Article 97, § 4, of Law No. 9,610 of 19 February 1998 (Copyright Law); no charge may be made where reciprocity is not assured.

§ 2. The holder of the remuneration right provided for in the chapeau who opts for direct negotiation and authorization, under the terms of item I of the chapeau, may exercise it regardless of subsequent regulation.

Article 66. The use of image, audio, voice or video content that portrays or identifies natural persons by AI systems shall respect personality rights, as provided for in Law No. 10,406, of January 10, 2002 (Civil Code), and in the relevant legislation.

paragraphs: Article 67. Sectoral authorities shall define different criteria for AI systems offered by micro-enterprises, small businesses and startups that promote the development of the national technology industry.
Sole paragraph. Differentiated criteria shall consider the competitive impact of related economic activities, the number of users affected and the nature of the economic activities carried out.

Article 67. Sectoral authorities shall define different criteria for AI systems offered by micro-enterprises, small businesses and startups that promote the development of the national technology industry.

paragraphs: Article 68. The following constitute guidelines for the actions of the Union, the States, the Federal District and the Municipalities in the development of AI in Brazil:

I – establishment of transparent, collaborative, democratic, multi-stakeholder and multi-sector governance mechanisms, with the participation of government, the business sector, the third sector, and academia, with particular consideration for vulnerable groups;
II – promoting trust in AI technologies, with the dissemination of information and knowledge about their ethical and responsible uses;
III – encouraging training and preparing people for the restructuring of the job market;

IV – promotion of interoperability between AI systems, including between different federal levels and different sectors of society, with decisions that seek to avoid technological dependence and promote the continued use of developed or contracted systems;
V – public disclosure and dissemination of data in an open, structured, and secure manner;
VI – environmental protection and sustainable development;
VII – promoting international cooperation, by encouraging the sharing of knowledge about AI systems, in order to facilitate regulatory and technological interoperability;

VIII – promotion of investment in AI aimed at solving the country's problems, promoting its socioeconomic, cultural and environmental development, and for the development of the national and regional production system, valuing the country's technological autonomy and its insertion and competitiveness in the domestic and international markets.
Article 69. AI systems used by public authorities shall seek to ensure:

I – accessibility of people, regardless of their physical-motor, perceptual, sensory, intellectual, mental, cultural and social capabilities, safeguarding aspects of confidentiality and administrative and legal restrictions;
II – compatibility with both human reading and automated processing of information;
III – ease of use of e-government services that use AI systems;
IV – guarantee of transparency regarding the use of AI systems;
V – promotion of Portuguese culture and language;
VI – encouraging the development of national AI systems, with special attention to national issues and cultural, language and socioeconomic context nuances.
Article 70. The public administration, within the scope of the Union, the States, the Federal District and the Municipalities, shall implement programs to:
I – education, training, capacity-building, and technical and higher-level qualification and reskilling in AI, aligned with the needs of the market and the public sector;
II – digital literacy for meaningful, responsible and equitable use of available AI systems, prioritizing basic education;

III – support for workers impacted and possibly affected by the adoption of AI, with a focus on promoting well-being, requalification, adaptation to new job market demands and professional reintegration;
IV – awareness and training in sustainability in the field of advanced digital technologies, with an emphasis on responsible practices in the use of resources; and

V – encouraging educational institutions to include subjects on environmental impact and sustainability in the development and operation of AI systems and applications and other advanced digital technologies in their curricula.
§ 1. The programs referred to in the chapeau shall seek to reduce inequalities, especially between the different regions of the country.

§ 2. The literacy measures referred to in item II of the chapeau shall include basic notions and skills about AI systems and their functioning, including the different types of products and uses, their risks and their benefits.
Article 71. The State shall periodically formulate and promote studies, as well as set goals, strategies, plans and schedules, relating to the use and development of AI in the Country.
units.fullText: Article 71. The State shall periodically formulate and promote studies, as well as set goals, strategies, plans and schedules, relating to the use and development of AI in the Country.

paragraphs: Article 72. The rights and principles expressed in this Law do not exclude others provided for in the national legal system or in international treaties to which the Federative Republic of Brazil is a party.
units.fullText: Article 72. The rights and principles expressed in this Law do not exclude others provided for in the national legal system or in international treaties to which the Federative Republic of Brazil is a party.

paragraphs: Article 73. In order to promote national technological development, the SIA shall regulate simplified regimes, involving relaxation of regulatory obligations provided for in this Law, in the following cases:
I – open and free standards and formats, with the exception of those considered high risk or that fall under Section V of Chapter IV of this Law;
II – national promotion;

III – encouraging innovation and scientific and technological research in the productive environment, with a view to technological training, achieving technological autonomy and the development of the country's national and regional production system;
IV – projects of public interest and those that meet the priorities of industrial and science, technology and innovation policies and that are related to solving Brazilian problems;

V – projects carried out in public-private partnerships, or in strategic partnerships, in a Scientific, Technological and Innovation Institution (ICT), Technological Innovation Center (NIT), support foundation, technology parks or technology hubs.
Sole paragraph. The Executive Branch shall provide economic incentives in the cases provided for in the chapeau of this article.
Article 74. In accordance with Chapter IX of this Law, the Executive Branch:

I – shall provide, within 2 (two) years, the ANPD with the resources necessary for its work, including its administrative restructuring, in order to ensure legal certainty and efficiency in supervising and monitoring compliance with this Law;
II – shall define the list of bodies and entities that shall act as the sectoral authorities forming part of the SIA;
III – shall define the detailed composition of Cria;
IV – shall define the activities of the Federal Public Administration Mediation and Conciliation Chamber; and
V – shall define the composition and powers of Cecia.
Article 75. The chapeau of Article 3 of Law No. 14,533, of January 11, 2023 (National Digital Education Policy), comes into force with the addition of the following item VI:
VI – critical algorithmic literacy and critical computing, which involves reading about the social and human implications of technologies, especially considering artificial intelligence.

paragraphs: Article 79. In view of the impact of technological transformation, every four years the SIA shall conduct studies and issue an opinion to be submitted to the National Congress concerning the need to improve the standards established in this Law.

paragraphs: Article 80. This Law comes into force 730 (seven hundred and thirty) days after its official publication.

I – Article 13;

§ 2. The following come into force on the date of publication of this Law:
I – the provisions of Chapter IX, with the exception of Article 50;
II – the following Sections of Chapter X:
a) Section III; and
b) Section V.
